title: Provide an Android APK build for the expense tracker

requirements.text: Add Android packaging support so the web app can be built and installed as an Android APK (WebView wrapper) using a standard approach (e.g., Capacitor) that works with a React + Vite-style frontend.

quotes: can you make an APK version so that I can easly download in my android phone

acceptanceCriteria: Repo includes Android packaging configuration and an Android project scaffold suitable for building an APK from the existing frontend.
A developer can run documented commands to build a debug APK locally (or in CI) without manual Android Studio project setup beyond installing Android SDK prerequisites.
The Android app loads the deployed Internet Computer frontend URL or the bundled web assets and renders the existing routes (/ dashboard, /add, /expenses, /settings) without blank screens.

requirements.text: Ensure Internet Identity authentication flow works from the Android APK (including any required WebView allow-list/navigation handling), and document any user steps needed to complete login on Android.

quotes: can you make an APK version so that I can easly download in my android phone

acceptanceCriteria: From the Android APK, a user can initiate login via Internet Identity and return to the app in an authenticated state.
Expense CRUD operations still work from the Android APK after authentication (add, edit, delete, list).
Any required Android network/navigation configuration (e.g., allowing identity and canister domains) is included in the repo and described in documentation.

requirements.text: Add clear developer documentation for producing an installable APK and sharing it (debug build and optional release signing), including prerequisites, build commands, and where the APK artifact is output.

quotes: can you make an APK version so that I can easly download in my android phone

acceptanceCriteria: Documentation exists in-repo (e.g., README section) with step-by-step instructions to generate an APK.
Docs specify how to install the APK onto a physical Android device (ADB install) and any Android settings needed (e.g., allow unknown sources).
Docs explain how the APK points to the app (bundled assets vs remote URL) and how to change the target URL if needed.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui (read-only UI component sources).
Do not modify frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts / .tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, or frontend/src/main.tsx (immutable paths). Compose around these hooks if changes are needed.
Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo; do not introduce additional backend services.
nonGoals: Implementing native Android-only features (push notifications, GPS, camera, background sync).
Publishing to Google Play Store or setting up Play Console listings.
Adding third-party authentication providers beyond Internet Identity.
